$comment: Copy to config.json and edit for your install. These values populate NID templates, default capabilities, and budget defaults.
$comment_issuer: Domain fragment in NIDs: urn:nps:agent:{issuer_domain}:{agent_id}. 'dev.localhost' matches @nps-kit/identity DevCA. Override in production with your Org CA's domain.
$comment_issuer_agent: The agent_id that issued/dispatched tasks. main-agent, orchestrator, dispatcher, or whatever your orchestrator calls itself.
$comment_budget: Default per-task NPT cap. NPT = NPS Token (cross-model standardized unit, NPS-0 §4.3). Override per-dispatch via --budget. Calibrated for Sonnet; see model_rates below.

category_budget_npt.$comment: Per-category NPT caps. At Sonnet npt_usd rate ($0.000025/NPT), these derive to the USD targets in category_usd_cap below.

category_usd_cap.$comment: USD ceiling per task category. Takes precedence over the NPT-derived ceiling in dispatch. Set to match your actual budget policy. Includes the nop_overhead_usd dispatch cost.

$comment_overhead: Estimated USD cost of the dispatch scaffolding itself (worktree creation, Claude Code startup, intent/result I/O). Added to the category_usd_cap budget for the --max-budget-usd ceiling when category_usd_cap is not set.

model_rates.$comment: Actual API rates per model, per token channel (MTok = 1 million tokens). Used for accurate post-hoc NPT cost reporting (NPS-0 §4.3). npt_usd is the NPT-to-USD multiplier used to derive --max-budget-usd when no category_usd_cap is set.

sonnet.$comment: Sonnet calibration: 40K NPT → $1.00, 60K NPT → $1.50 (code/docs/refactor/ops). research uses category_usd_cap ($2.00) because research tasks are output-heavy and exceed the base rate.

haiku.$comment: Haiku calibration: 40K NPT → $0.20, 60K NPT → $0.30. Well within typical task ceilings.

opus.$comment: Opus calibration: 40K NPT → $5.00. Opus tasks should use explicit --budget overrides.
